and weekly schedule

commit.message:
fix: inject agents into AbstractSearchOrchestrator to resolve NPE

keywordGeneratorAgent, snippetExtensionAgent, summaryContextAgent,
searchResultsAssessmentAgent and contextOrchestrator were declared as
protected fields in AbstractSearchOrchestrator but never populated,
because the orchestrators are instantiated via 'new' inside @Provides
methods — Dagger does not inject fields of objects it doesn't manage.

Changes:
- AbstractSearchOrchestrator: add initAgents() method to wire all agent deps

The previous code set CMAKE_DISABLE_FIND_PACKAGE_OpenCV=ON which
prevented find_package(OpenCV) from running, even when the workflow
passes -DOpenCV_DIR pointing to C:\tools\opencv\build. The vcpkg
fallback then failed (no vcpkg OpenCV installed), resulting in
OpenCV_FOUND=OFF and fatal 'opencv2/opencv.hpp' not found errors.

falls back gracefully if not found.

commit.message:
fix(network): fix 4G relay reservation dispatch (v0.1.33)

- Remove relay_server from client MetaverseBehaviour — clients should
  not relay for others; this eliminates the relay::server 10s idle timer
  that was keeping connections alive just long enough to mask the real bug

- Defer listen_via_relay from ConnectionEstablished to Identify::Received
  — Identify fires after libp2p deduplicates multiple transports (TCP+WS),
  so the reservation request goes to the surviving connection's handler,
  not the one that gets closed by dedup

- Add circuit_registered: HashSet<PeerId> to track which relay peers have
  an active circuit reservation. Guards against duplicate listen_via_relay
  calls and cleared on ConnectionClosed (num_established == 0)

- Increase idle_connection_timeout from 60s to 300s as safety net

- Update connect_to_bootstrap re-registration to filter by circuit_registered

Root cause: libp2p-relay 0.21.1 priv_client.rs uses .first() to select
which connection handler receives a listen_on dispatch. Both TCP and WS
circuit listen_on calls landed on the TCP handler (connected first).
When TCP closed due to dedup, both reservations were lost. WS handler
had reservation=None so connection_keep_alive() returned false.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: align cross-module interfaces for fences, headings, and nav scroll

Three interface mismatches fixed:

1. markdown_fence.rs + highlight.rs: accepted arbitrarily-indented fences
   and headings (trim_start strips all whitespace). CommonMark limits ATX
   heading and code fence indentation to 0-3 spaces. Lines with 4+ spaces
   are indented code blocks, not headings/fences. This caused highlight.rs
   to color code blocks as headings, and format.rs to preserve trailing
   whitespace inside pseudo-fences that pulldown_cmark never recognizes.

2. render.rs heading_y vs nav_outline: heading_y counted ALL Block::Heading
   variants (including empty ones like '# '), but nav_outline::extract_headings
   skips empty headings. This ordinal mismatch caused preview_nav_scroll_y
   to navigate to wrong positions when documents contained empty headings.
   Fixed heading_y to skip empty headings, aligning with nav_outline.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
